mapping.a_element: Brazier instability: a critical bending load beyond which the cross-section collapses
mapping.b_element: percolation tipping point beyond which the giant connected component collapses

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic notion of a critical transition; the actual machinery is different — paper A solves continuum elasticity with contact mechanics via a hybrid material point method to capture geometric-elastic buckling of slender structures, whereas paper B combines a Hawkes self-exciting point process for conflict intensity with cascade/percolation on a road graph, so no role-for-role mapping of governing equations or state variables holds.
